arts.ttl: January signing fit again for Middlesbrough at Stoke City but seven players ruled out
arts.sum: Michael Carrick will still be without key loan forward Ben Doak, however, although the Liverpool loanee is hoping to join back in training next week after missing the last four games. Michael Carrick told GazetteLive: “None of the injured lads will be back, but Ryan has recovered from illness over the course of the weekend and he’s back in and around it, which is good.I don’t expect the injured lads to be back for the weekend either, they’re a little bit further away than that.

arts.ttl: Manhoef starts: Stoke City team news and predicted XI to face Middlesbrough
arts.sum: The latest Stoke City team news as Mark Robins' side gear up to face Middlesbrough in the Championship. The post Manhoef starts: Stoke City team news and predicted XI to face Middlesbrough appeared first on The72 - Football League News.

arts.ttl: Million Manhoef on a year at Stoke City and changing team mindset
arts.sum: It’s 40 games, nine goals, three managers, 40 team-mates, one annoying injury and two battles at the wrong end of the division since the 23-year-old winger joined Stoke City from Vitesse Arnhem mid-way through last season. “It’s hard for the whole team or the whole of Stoke to think the same, of course, but that’s what I’m trying to say to the guys, ‘What if we win 13 games in a row?

arts.ttl: ‘Everyone’s waiting’ – Middlesbrough boss issues update on Liverpool loanee’s injury recovery
arts.sum: Middlesbrough hope Ben Doak can be ‘in and around’ training again next week, Michael Carrick has said. Middlesbrough… The post ‘Everyone’s waiting’ – Middlesbrough boss issues update on Liverpool loanee’s injury recovery appeared first on The72 - Football League News.

arts.ttl: Positive Middlesbrough injury updates on Ryan Giles and Ben Doak ahead of key run in
arts.sum: Ryan Giles will be the only Middlesbrough player returning to the squad on Tuesday evening for the clash with Stoke City, but there was a positive update on the return of Ben Doak too. And appreciating the clamour for the 19-year-old’s return, Carrick said ahead of Stoke: “None of the injured lads will be back, but Ryan has recovered from illness over the course of the weekend and he’s back in and around it, which is good.I don’t expect the injured lads to be back for the weekend either, they’re a little bit further away than that.

arts.ttl: Bench boys on standby in Stoke City predicted starting XI vs Middlesbrough
arts.sum: There will be some juggling with game time as Mark Robins welcomes back key players like Sam Gallagher, Ben Wilmot and Million Manhoef like he already has done with Ben Pearson. Pearson has played an hour in the last two matches, for example, while Manhoef and Gallagher got half-an-hour and 15 minutes respectively last time out.

arts.ttl: Middlesbrough told to keep embattled Michael Carrick ahead of Stoke City clash
arts.sum: They have a game in hand on the top six, however, to be played at Stoke City on Tuesday (7.45pm) and McAnuff told BBC's Football Daily 72+: The EFL Podcast he is adamant he deserves more time - and still isn't ruling out promotion this season. Championship pundit Jobi McAnuff insists the Middlesbrough board should ignore growing pressure and keep their faith in Michael Carrick until at least the end of the season.

arts.ttl: Kelechi Iheanacho doubt cast after 'disruptive' Middlesbrough January transfer
arts.sum: Sam Parkin has cast doubt on Kelechi Iheanacho being able to replace the goals that Middlesbrough have lost after the sale of Emmanuel Latte Lath. But there were times where he didn’t look like a definitive goal threat like they had with Latte Lath or the general play he provided across the last 18 months."

arts.ttl: Honest Mark Robins reflects on whirlwind first six weeks at Stoke City and most important question
arts.sum: There have been 11 matches, a transfer window when the top scorer was recalled, injuries to key players, players returning from long-term absences, Financial Fair Play challenges, coaches leaving, arriving or being moved, a head of recruitment going, a relegation fight to navigate, long mid-week trips, home form to transform and a goal problem to resolve. So, yes, Robins took a breath when the last question at his post-match press conference at Norwich on Saturday asked how pleased he was about the job he had taken on and how equipped Stoke are to pull away from the bottom.

arts.ttl: Stoke City set to be without five players vs Middlesbrough as Mark Robins waits on key man
arts.sum: The returning players have meant that Bosun Lawal could afford to get valuable game time with the under-21s rather than sit on the first team bench. It would be a boost if he was available for a mid-week clash with Middlesbrough at the bet365 Stadium (Tuesday, 7.45pm) and a test against Watford on Saturday (3pm) as other key players start to return for selection.
